title: US CPI Forecast Snapshot
summary: June 2026 CPI is expected to show headline cooling as the energy shock unwinds, with core remaining sticky. WTI crude collapsed from ~$102 to ~$70 since May, dragging gasoline prices down ~20%. The headline MoM should fall toward +0.2% from +0.5% in May. Core services and shelter are still the main risks to a clean disinflation print. The Fed held rates at 3.50-3.75% on June 17 and the dot plot showed 9 members favoring at least one hike this year, keeping the macro backdrop hawkish.
baseCase: Inline-to-cool headline with sticky core CPI as energy drag offsets services persistence

release.name: CPI
release.thesis: Headline inflation cools as the Iran-energy shock fades. WTI down ~30% from peak and gasoline down ~20% should drag the energy component negative. Core remains sticky on shelter and services, limiting the dovish readthrough. The Fed is more focused on core than headline.

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.outcome: Headline +0.2% MoM, core +0.25% MoM — energy unwind dominates, core sticky
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Rates drift lower on headline relief but core limits the move. Growth equities supported, duration rallies modestly. USD softens.
scenarios.invalidation: Core services re-accelerate above +0.35% MoM or shelter jumps back to +0.4%

scenarios.label: Hotter inflation surprise
scenarios.outcome: Core CPI prints +0.35% MoM or higher despite headline softness
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Rates spike, USD rallies, growth and small-cap equities under pressure. Fed hike odds for December rise above 70%.
scenarios.invalidation: Sticky core is driven by one-off components like airline fares or medical care

scenarios.label: Cooler disinflation impulse
scenarios.outcome: Core CPI undershoots at +0.15% MoM or lower with headline near flat
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Fed cut odds re-price for late 2026. Duration rallies hard, growth equities rip, DXY weakens.
scenarios.invalidation: Market already priced a benign outcome and the move is faded quickly

setups.bias: Long duration on cooler or inline headline
setups.trigger: Headline CPI <= +0.2% MoM and core stays below +0.3% MoM
setups.invalidation: Core services re-accelerate with wages or OER ticking higher

setups.bias: Risk-on if rates fade after the print
setups.trigger: 10Y yield fails to hold above 4.40% after release
setups.invalidation: Hot core CPI with hawkish Fed repricing sends yields toward 4.60%

setups.trigger: Core CPI >= +0.35% MoM with headline also firm
setups.invalidation: Rates move lower despite the beat as energy drag dominates the narrative

watchItems.label: Fed path
watchItems.note: Dot plot showed 9 members for at least one hike. The market is pricing ~60% odds of a December hike.

watchItems.label: 10Y UST
watchItems.note: Yield has pulled back from 4.60% highs as oil crashed. Key regime line for equity duration sensitivity.

watchItems.label: Oil / WTI
watchItems.note: Down from $102 in May. The biggest swing factor for headline CPI. If it holds here, July CPI will also be soft.

keyDrivers: Energy shock unwind: WTI collapsed ~30% from May peak. Gasoline down ~20%. This is the dominant headline story.
Core services remain sticky: shelter +0.3% MoM, transportation services +0.4%, medical care +0.3% in May.
Fed policy shift: the June 17 FOMC removed easing bias. Warsh is focused on trimmed-mean inflation, not just headline.
risks: The market may already be positioned for a soft headline, limiting the upside reaction even if the print is cool.
A single hot core services component (shelter, medical care, airline fares) can reverse the rates move quickly.
Geopolitics: the US-Iran ceasefire is fragile. Any re-escalation would spike oil and invalidate the soft-CPI thesis for July and August.
